{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the trust-region problem\n\\[\n\\min_{p\\in\\mathbb{R}^n} m(p) \\stackrel{\\mathrm{def}}{=} f+g^T p+\\frac12 p^T Bp\n\\qquad \\text{subject to } \\|p\\|\\le \\Delta.\n\\tag{4.5}\n\\]\nSet\n\\[\ng=\\left(-\\frac{1}{\\epsilon},-1,-\\epsilon^2\\right)^T,\n\\]\nwhere \\(\\epsilon>0\\) is small, and let\n\\[\nB=\\operatorname{diag}\\left(\\frac{1}{\\epsilon^3},1,\\epsilon^3\\right),\n\\qquad \\Delta=0.5.\n\\]\nShow that the exact solution of (4.5) has components\n\\[\n\\bigl(O(\\epsilon),\\tfrac12+O(\\epsilon),O(\\epsilon)\\bigr)^T,\n\\]\nand that the corresponding reduction in the model \\(m\\) is\n\\[\n\\frac{3}{8}+O(\\epsilon).\n\\]\nFor the two-dimensional minimization strategy, show that the solution is a scalar multiple of \\(B^{-1}g\\) and that the reduction in \\(m\\) is \\(O(\\epsilon)\\)."}
